gaps.title: Telegram Account and Proxy Session Management
gaps.jtbd: When managing outreach accounts, I want to authenticate sessions via OTP/session files and route traffic through individual proxies, so that mass bans are prevented and stable account status is monitored.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a SOCKS5/HTTP proxy and Telegram credentials, When an account is authenticated, Then the backend routes all session interactions through the assigned proxy and updates its active status.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 1 (Session Manager) is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Campaign Management and Spintax Template Engine
gaps.jtbd: When configuring an outreach campaign, I want to upload contact lists and define message templates with spintax support, so that personalized random messages are automatically dispatched with rotation on flood limits.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a contact list CSV and a spintax template, When the campaign is launched, Then the system generates randomized message variations and rotates sending accounts upon hitting rate limits.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 2 (Campaign Manager) is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: AI Dialog Engine and Intent-Based Stop-Action Triggers
gaps.jtbd: When a prospect replies to an outreach message, I want the LLM Dialog Engine to auto-respond based on context and freeze on stop-action triggers, so that hot leads are qualified and seamlessly marked for human operator intervention.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given an incoming lead response, When processed by the Dialog Engine, Then the history context is saved, an LLM-generated reply is sent, and AI is paused if a stop-action trigger or replica limit is reached.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Module 3 (AI-Ассистент / LLM Dialog Engine) is completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Account Warming and Anti-Ban Strategy
gaps.jtbd: When operating automated Telegram accounts, I want to perform background warming actions and use non-linear delay intervals, so that the risk of automatic spam-detection blocks is minimized.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given a connected Telegram account, When background activity is enabled, Then the account automatically performs channel subscriptions and reading posts with exponentially distributed delays between tasks.
gaps.reason: literal brief requirement: Section 4 anti-ban features such as background warming and non-linear delays are completely missing from the task list.

gaps.title: Administrator Authentication and Session Management
gaps.jtbd: When logging into the platform, I want a secure authentication and session management mechanism, so that unauthorized users are blocked from accessing sensitive Telegram accounts and campaigns.
gaps.acceptanceCriteria: Given an unauthenticated request to the dashboard or API, When accessed, Then the system requires valid administrator login credentials, returning HTTP 401/403 or redirecting to login on failure.
gaps.reason: domain-standard expectation: A private admin panel with users and Spring Security in the backend must have secure session authentication to prevent unauthorized control of the network.
